HDFC Small Cap Fund is a small-cap mutual fund . As per SEBI’s classification, small-cap funds are required to invest at least 65% of their assets in small-cap companies, those ranked 251st and below by market capitalization.
The fund uses the BSE 250 Small Cap TRI as its benchmark, which helps measure its performance against a broad universe of similar small-cap stocks . The fund has a strong presence in sectors like Healthcare, Technology, Financials, Automobiles, and Infrastructure.
India offers a wide and fast-growing base of small-cap companies, which the fund house believes can deliver strong long-term returns. HDFC Small Cap Mutual Fund focuses on selecting fundamentally sound businesses with reasonable valuations, aiming to generate alpha while managing risk effectively.
Fund Manager of  HDFC Small Cap Fund, since 28 June 2014

Get key fund statistics, minimum investment details, AUM, expense ratio, exit load, and tax treatment.
HDFC Flexi Cap is a flexi-cap mutual fund launched on 1st January 1995. These funds follow a diversified equity investment strategy where they invest in companies across all market captialization and adjust allocations based on market conditions. The fund uses Nifty 500 TRI as its benchmark. Nifty 500 represents the broader market and helps the fund compare its returns with those of other companies
HDFC Mutual Fund follows a bottom-up approach , which involves paying attention to individual businesses instead of macroeconomic factors. They also pay close attention to Quantitative (Growth, Cash Flows, Risks) and Qualitative (Management, Track Record, Insights) aspects of the companies.
Staying true to the nature of the fund, they diversify across market caps and asset classes. Diversification is achieved through a balanced mix of asset types, loan sizes, contract volumes, and geographies, while risk in less diversified pools is mitigated using credit enhancements. HDFC Flexi Cap maintains a strong focus on industries like Financials, Automobiles, Healthcare, Technology, etc.

The SBI Contra Fund is an equity mutual fund from SBI Mutual Fund that invests in out-of-favour or undervalued stocks (a contrarian strategy) to achieve long-term capital appreciation. It employs a combination of top-down and bottom-up analysis to pick stocks across large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap segments. The strategy focuses on assets that are currently unpopular, aiming to profit when market sentiment changes.
The primary benchmark for the fund is the BSE 500 Total Return Index (TRI), and its secondary benchmark is the BSE SENSEX TRI. Currently, the Fund size (AUM) stands at approximately ₹49,218 Crore .
Fund Manager of  SBI Contra Fund, since 1 December 2023
Fund Manager of  SBI Contra Fund, since 7 May 2018

HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund is an equity fund. This fund was started on 1 January, 2013. The fund is managed by Amar Kalkundrikar, Dhruv Muchhal. The fund could potentially beat inflation in the long-run.
HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund has given a CAGR return of 14.75% since inception. Over the last 1, 3 and 5 years the fund has given a CAGR return of 12.94%, 20.76% and 19.32% respectively.
HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund has allocated its funds majorly in Cash Equivalent, Financial Services, Government, Consumer Cyclical, Tech, Health, Industrial, Basic Materials, Communication, Consumer Defensive, Energy, Utilities. Its top holdings are HDFC Bank Ltd, ICICI Bank Ltd, Axis Bank Ltd, SBI Life Insurance Co Ltd, Maruti Suzuki India Ltd
As it is a elss (tax savings) mutual fund the taxation is as follows: For short term (less than a year) capital gains will be taxed at 20% For long term (more than 1 year) capital gains will be taxed at 12.5% without indexation benefit Dividends will always be taxed at slab rate. Long term gains upto Rs 1 lakh are exempt capital gains tax.
To generate capital appreciation / income from a portfolio, comprising predominantly of equity & equity related instruments. There is no assurance that the investment objective of the Scheme will be realized.
Minimum investment for lump sum payment is INR 500.00 and for SIP is INR 500.00. There is a lock in period for HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund of 3 Years
Fund Manager of  HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund, since 8 December 2025
Fund Manager of  HDFC ELSS TaxSaver Fund, since 22 June 2023
